title: Enable and use move semantics for expensive types
description: Define or default move operations and pass-by-value-then-move so large objects transfer ownership cheaply instead of deep-copying.
body: Use when handling vectors, strings, buffers, or other heap-backed types in hot paths or sink functions. Do not std::move a const object (it silently copies), move a value you still need, or leave the rule of five half-implemented. Actions: =default move ctor/assignment when members are movable, take sink parameters by value and std::move them into members, return local objects by value to enable RVO/NRVO, and mark moved-from objects as only valid for assignment/destruction. Validation signals include a benchmark showing fewer allocations after enabling moves and ASan confirming no use-after-move. Risk boundary: using a moved-from object is UB for most types; if a type both copies and moves, verify the rule of five is complete before relying on either.
